task_completion.description: The agent ran a flight simulation in OpenRocket achieving an apogee close to the 415m target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket GUI 'Flight Simulations' tab is visible, showing a simulation run with an apogee value between 405m and 425m.

task_completion.description: A new motor configuration was created using an Aerotech I-class motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket GUI 'Motor configuration' tab shows an Aerotech I-class motor (e.g., I200W, I284W, I211W) assigned to the rocket.

task_completion.description: A second flight simulation was run for the new I-class motor configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket GUI 'Flight Simulations' tab shows a second simulation run (for the I-class motor) with a significantly higher apogee than the base motor.

task_completion.description: Time-series flight data for both simulations were exported to CSV files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows 'calibration_flight.csv' and 'prediction_flight.csv' located in /home/ga/Documents/exports/.

task_completion.description: The modified rocket design was saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows 'calibrated_rocket.ork' located in /home/ga/Documents/rockets/.

task_completion.description: A post-flight analysis report was written containing the required documentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the contents of 'calibration_report.txt' in /home/ga/Documents/exports/, mentioning the calibration method (e.g., surface finish, drag coefficient), the matched apogee (~415m), and the predicted apogee for the I-class motor.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the OpenRocket application open and being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands creating mock files.

integrity.description: The agent did not add physical mass or alter component dimensions to cheat the calibration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket component tree should not show newly added 'Mass component' items, and the rocket's visual shape/length should remain consistent with the original design.

integrity.description: The CSV files were generated via OpenRocket's export feature, not manually mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV files are opened or previewed, they should contain standard OpenRocket time-series headers (e.g., Time, Altitude, Velocity) and dense numerical data, not just a few manually typed lines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target calibration apogee is exactly 415m with a strict tolerance of +/- 10m. The modified rocket must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/calibrated_rocket.ork. The CSV exports must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/exports/calibration_flight.csv and /home/ga/Documents/exports/prediction_flight.csv. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/exports/calibration_report.txt. The agent must not add artificial mass components or alter launch conditions to achieve the target apogee.
